_status: partially populated (2026-07-07 second research pass) - see data-requirements.md §17 and ISSUES.md ISS-9

purchaseCost.note: 2022 government tender (Indian Embassy, Nepal): 200 hemodialysis machines + 60 RO systems, total INR 23.49 crore, implying INR 11.5 lakh/machine. Caveats: (1) bulk-procurement price (200 units) -- a private hospital buying 1-2 units may pay more; (2) the total bundles RO/water-treatment systems across the machines, so this per-machine figure likely includes some pro-rata site/RO cost, not a bare machine-only price.

usefulLifeYears.note: Companies Act Schedule II's only other category besides the named diagnostic-scan class (Cat-scan/ultrasound, 13yr) is 'other medical/surgical equipment' at 15 years. Dialysis machines aren't individually named by S8, but as therapeutic (not imaging-diagnostic) equipment they fall in this second category by elimination -- a direct inference from S8's own two-way split, not a separate research claim. See data-requirements.md §12.4/§14.

_usagePerDayRange.note: MoHFW/NITI official planning guideline: three 4-hour shifts/machine/day = 3 sessions/machine/day. This is a DESIGN-CAPACITY norm, not necessarily real-world utilization -- the same source notes real-world utilization 'may be lower in smaller facilities'.

billedTariffPerUse.note: Hemodialysis (seronegative) CGHS reimbursement ceiling -- a government-scheme floor, NOT a private cash tariff, from a different-vintage CGHS document (nominally 2014, updated 2024-02-05) than the imaging tariffs elsewhere in this file. Seropositive sessions price higher (~1650/1898), sustained low-efficiency lower (~1250/1438). A private-provider source (S19, from the original pass) gives ~1500-4000/session patient-cost content but explicitly cautions against use as a default -- not used here.

launchDelayMonths.note: Confirmed unavailable across two research passes -- no credible source reports purchase-to-first-revenue duration for dialysis machines. Installation is mainly plumbing/water-treatment/staff training, but no duration figure was found.
